title: Genetic Counseling: MedlinePlus
content: Genetic Counseling On this page Basics Summary Start Here Learn More Related Issues Specifics See, Play and Learn No links available Research Clinical Trials Journal Articles Resources Reference Desk Find an Expert For You Children Women Patient Handouts Summary Genetic counseling provides information and support to people who have, or may be at risk for, genetic disorders . A genetic counselor meets with you to discuss genetic risks. The counseling may be for yourself or a family member. Or you may get it when you are planning or expecting a baby. You may follow up with genetic testing . There are many reasons to seek genetic counseling. You may consider it if you : Have a personal or family history of a genetic condition or birth defect Are pregnant or planning to be pregnant after age 35 Already have a child with a genetic disorder or birth defect Have had two or more pregnancy losses or a baby who died Have had ultrasound or screening tests that suggest a possible problem Start Here Genetic Counseling (Centers for Disease Control and Prevention) Genetic Counseling (For Parents) (Nemours Foundation) Also in Spanish Genetic Counseling FAQ (National Human Genome Research Institute) What happens during a genetic consultation?: MedlinePlus Genetics (National Library of Medicine) Related Issues About Genetic Counselors (National Society of Genetic Counselors) Also in Spanish Genetics: MedlinePlus Genetics (National Library of Medicine) Also in Spanish What Is Genetics?
